learningObjective: Apply the FTC to calculate definite integrals using F(b) - F(a) - connecting the symbolic calculation to the geometric meaning students have built
ahaMoment: Discovering that the vertical measurement F(b)-F(a) on one curve mysteriously equals the horizontal area measurement under a completely different curve
targetMisconception: Students struggle to connect symbolic calculation to the geometric meaning

miniSpec.title: Split-Screen Symbolic and Geometric Comparison
miniSpec.narrative_wrapper: The screen divides into symbolic work on the left and graph on the right. Students drag terms in the symbolic calculation (writing out ∫f(x)dx = F(x)|ᵇₐ = F(b) - F(a)) and watch corresponding visual elements highlight on the graph. Rearranging the algebra animates the geometry.
miniSpec.manipulated_variable: Draggable mathematical expression tiles that students arrange in proper order: integral symbol, function, antiderivative, evaluation bars, substituted values, subtraction
miniSpec.primary_visual_feedback: As each symbolic piece is placed correctly, the corresponding geometric feature pulses or highlights on the graph: placing F(x) shows the antiderivative curve, placing evaluation notation highlights points (a,F(a)) and (b,F(b)), placing subtraction shows the difference
miniSpec.goal_structure: Correct sequencing task: student arranges symbolic steps in correct order to complete the calculation, with the final arrangement unlocking an animation showing why the symbolic result equals the geometric area
miniSpec.causal_chain_exposed: Connection lines dynamically link: the integral symbol to the shaded area, F(x) to the antiderivative curve, evaluation notation to specific points, and the subtraction F(b)-F(a) to both the vertical distance on F(x) and the shaded area under f(x)
miniSpec.trace_strategy: Persistent bidirectional links: colored lines remain visible connecting each symbolic element to its geometric counterpart, creating a web of correspondences that remains throughout interaction
miniSpec.aha_moment: Discovering that the vertical measurement F(b)-F(a) on one curve mysteriously equals the horizontal area measurement under a completely different curve

interactionContract.student_changes: scrubs values of a and b in the formula and sees the calculation update
interactionContract.student_sees: the formula F(b) - F(a) updates alongside the visual shaded area, confirming the symbolic and geometric answers match

modelVariables.calcIntegrals_a: left bound of integration (frozen/locked from previous section), inherited
modelVariables.calcIntegrals_b: right bound of integration, student-scrubbable in formula
modelVariables.calcIntegrals_f: the speed function type (frozen from previous section), inherited
modelVariables.calcIntegrals_Fa: derived: F(a) = antiderivative evaluated at a
modelVariables.calcIntegrals_Fb: derived: F(b) = antiderivative evaluated at b
modelVariables.calcIntegrals_difference: derived: F(b) - F(a), the definite integral result
modelVariables.calcIntegrals_area: derived: the shaded area under f(x) from a to b, equals calcIntegrals_difference
modelVariables.calcIntegrals_activeHighlight: string: which element is currently highlighted (integral, antiderivative, eval-a, eval-b, difference)

assertions.description: On load, the formula shows the integral with bounds, the graph shows both curves and the shaded area, but the connection between symbolic and geometric is not yet fully animated - posing the challenge of understanding why F(b)-F(a) gives the area
assertions.visualCheck: both f(x) and F(x) curves visible, shaded area under f(x), formula displays integral with scrubbable bounds

assertions.description: The upper bound (b) can be scrubbed and updates the calculation
assertions.visualCheck: upper bound marker moves to x=5, shaded area expands, F(b) value updates, difference calculation updates

assertions.description: The F(b)-F(a) calculation matches the geometric area under the curve
assertions.visualCheck: the formula result matches the displayed area value - symbolic and geometric answers are the same

assertions.description: The lower bound (a) is carried over from previous section and cannot be changed by the student (frozen at 0)
assertions.visualCheck: lower bound a is displayed but not interactive - shown as a fixed value

assertions.description: The vertical distance F(b)-F(a) on the antiderivative curve equals the horizontal area under the original curve
assertions.visualCheck: vertical bar showing F(b)-F(a) on antiderivative curve has same length as the accumulated area under original curve

invariants.description: The lower bound a is always less than the upper bound b
The symbolic F(b)-F(a) always equals the geometric area
F(b) - F(a) equals the difference variable
